title: Automation to copy people value from a mirror people column
excerpt:
as the title says, I need to copy a value from a people mirror column to an other people column. 
I tried several automations that could work but none had the option to update people, I need the automation to update the …

posts.author: zionmesil (zion)
posts.cookedHtml:
as the title says, I need to copy a value from a people mirror column to an other people column.
I tried several automations that could work but none had the option to update people, I need the automation to update the field based on the value in the mirror people column.
please help me find the automation that could help.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Is it a column that changes a lot or do youjust need the connection when the item is created?

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

If you are open to using 3rd party app you may find Column to Column app useful.

The app introduce following automations to copy data from mirror column to standard column:

posts.author: zionmesil (zion)
posts.cookedHtml: I need it when an item is created but also need the ability to change the person based on the person incharge in mirror since when 1 change the other boards done change automatically.

posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi all! Also interested by this feature. Did someone find a solution without using 3rd party app?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
It could be done with the workflow center. ie When person changes, find connected item and then update the connected item.

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Zion, I’ve run into the same limitation before Monday automations can’t directly update a people column from a mirror column. A common workaround is to use Integromat/Make or Zapier to watch the mirror column and then update the other people column automatically. It adds an extra step, but it works reliably until Monday natively supports this.

posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Desiree! Thanks for your feedback. The issue here is that my trigger is not a person changing but an email changing. And based on the new email, retrieving the good people and update another people field accordingly.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
You could also try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

It allows you to add the same item to multiple boards, and you could also share the people column between items on different boards.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
I used ‘people changing’ as just a random trigger. But you can use ‘column changes’ and choose the email column as the trigger.
